title: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles

seo.title: How to Make Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Fideo Seco is a cherished Mexican dish featuring short, thin noodles toasted to a golden crispness and then simmered in a rich, flavorful tomato broth. This comforting 'dry' pasta dish is a staple in many Mexican homes, offering a delightful texture and hearty taste. Perfect as a main course or a savory side, it embodies the warmth and tradition of authentic Mexican cuisine.

instructions.title: Prepare the Fideo Noodles
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the fideo noodles and toast them, stirring constantly, until they turn a deep golden brown. This usually takes about 3-5 minutes. Be careful not to burn them. Remove the toasted noodles from the skillet and set aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In a blender, combine the chopped Roma tomatoes, roughly chopped white onion, peeled garlic cloves, and 1 cup of the chicken broth. Blend until very smooth. If the mixture is too thick, add a splash more broth to help it blend.

instructions.title: Cook the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Return the skillet to medium heat. Carefully pour the blended tomato mixture into the skillet. Bring it to a simmer and cook for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly and deepens in color. This step helps to intensify the flavor.

instructions.title: Add Broth and Seasonings
instructions.text: Pour in the remaining 2 cups of chicken broth into the simmering sauce. Add the salt and ground cumin. Stir well to combine all the ingredients. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil.

instructions.title: Simmer the Fideo
instructions.text: Add the toasted fideo noodles back into the skillet with the broth and sauce. Stir gently to ensure all the noodles are submerged. Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the noodles are tender and most of the liquid has been absorbed.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once the fideo is cooked and the liquid is absorbed, remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld and the noodles to finish absorbing any remaining liquid. Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped cilantro, a dollop of Crema Mexicana or sour cream, and crumbled Cotija cheese.

tips: Always toast your fideo noodles to a deep golden brown; this step is crucial for developing a nutty flavor and preventing them from becoming mushy.
For a spicier kick, add one or two canned chipotle peppers in adobo sauce to the blender with the tomatoes and onion.
Fideo Seco is excellent served with a sprinkle of fresh cheese like cotija or queso fresco, a dollop of Mexican crema, and a side of refried beans or arroz rojo.
article: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles is a beloved Mexican Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
